metric","user":{"login":"nickrobinson251","id":13448787,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNDQ4Nzg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13448787?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251","html_url":"https://github.com/nickrobinson251","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Close https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47351 (builds on top of https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48416)

Adds to `Task` a metric for scheduled CPU time and wall time
- cpu time = amount of time we were actually running this task (according to our scheduler). Note: currently inclusive of GC time, but would be good to be able to separate that out (in a future PR)
-  wall time = amount of time between the scheduler becoming aware of this task and the task entering a terminal state (i.e. done or failed). 

I have put `record_cpu_time!` in `wait()`, so there is a singular place this is recorded by the scheduler, plus in `yield(t)`, `yieldto(t)` and `throwto(t)` (which skip the scheduler). `Channel`, `ReentrantLock`, `Event`, `Timer`, `Semaphore` are all implemented in terms of `wait(Condition)`, which in turn calls `wait()`. And `LibuvStream` similarly calls `wait()`. So hopefully this captures everything (albeit, slightly over-counting task CPU time by including any enqueuing work done before we hit `wait()`).

The various metrics counters could be a separate inlined struct if we think that's a useful abstraction, but for now i've just put them directly in `jl_task_t`. They are all atomic, except the `metrics_enabled` flag itself (which we now have to check on task start/switch/done even if metrics are not enabled) which is set on task construction and marked `const` on the julia side.

I'd love to add these metrics in a way that is extendable, so that in future PRs we could add more per-task metrics (e.g. compilation time, GC time, allocations, potentially a wait-time breakdown (time waiting on locks, channels, in the scheduler run queue, etc.), potentially count the number of times it yielded). 

Perhaps in future there could be ways to enable this on a per-thread and per-task basis. And potentially in future these same timings could be used by `@time` (e.g. writing this same timing data to a ScopedValue like in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55103 but only for tasks lexically scoped to inside the `@time` block).

Timings are off be default but can be turned on globally via starting julia with `--task-metrics=yes` or calling `Base.Experimental.task_metrics(true)`. Metrics are collected for all tasks created when metrics are enabled. In other words, enabling/disabling timings via `Base.Experimental.task_metrics` does not affect existing `Task`s, only new `Task`s.

The other new APIs are `Base.Experimental.task_cpu_time_ns(::Task)` and `Base.Experimental.task_wall_time_ns(::Task)` for retrieving the new metrics. These are safe to call on any task (including the current task, or a task running on another thread). Names are up for discussion. I've put these all in `Base.Experimental` to give us room to change up the APIs as we add more metrics in future PRs (without worrying about release timelines).

the bug 🐞**

I think there may be a regression in compilation times `solve(::ODEProblem)` with `StaticArrays` types. I have a minimum reproducible example below. First a `Vector` type is used, and that compiles quickly. Next an `MVector` type is used, and the `solve` call takes approximately one hour to complete; I haven't confirmed this locally (the call hangs, and I wait a few minutes before killing the process), but in CI the call _does_ finish, but it takes around one hour.

I don't have any specific package versions for when this worked, but I was able to run `solve` with `MVector` types in the last year. Here is [one example](https://github.com/cadojo/AstrodynamicalSolvers.jl/actions/runs/9392873337/job/25867900856) from 5 months ago; the full job completed in 13 minutes. 

**Expected behavior**

The `solve` call with `MVector` should compile and evaluate within an order of magnitude of `Vector`.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**


```julia
import Pkg
Pkg.activate(tempdir())
Pkg.add("StaticArrays")
Pkg.add("OrdinaryDiffEq")

using StaticArrays
using OrdinaryDiffEq

μ = 0.012150584395829193
u = [
    0.8222791798525636
    0.0
    0.0
    0.0
    0.13799313228400034
    0.0
]

function r2bp(du, u, p, t)
    x, y, z, ẋ, ẏ, ż = u
    μ, = p

    r = sqrt(x^2 + y^2 + z^2)

    du[1] = ẋ
    du[2] = ẏ
    du[3] = ż
    du[4] = μ * x / r^3
    du[5] = μ * y / r^3
    du[6] = μ * z / r^3

    return nothing
end


@info "Solving Problem with Vector Input"
@warn "This should compile in a couple of seconds."
problem = ODEProblem(r2bp, u, (0.0, 3.0), (μ,))
@time solution = solve(problem, Vern9(), reltol = 1e-12, abstol = 1e-12)

@info "Solving Problem with MVector State"
@error "This will take very long to compile!"
problem = ODEProblem(r2bp, MVector{6}(u), (0.0, 3.0), (μ,))
@time solution = solve(problem, Vern9(), reltol = 1e-12, abstol = 1e-12)
```

PR allows generated functions to return a `CodeInstance` containing optimized IR, allowing them to bypass inference and directly adding inferred code into the ordinary course of execution. This is an enabling capability for various external compiler implementations that may want to provide compilation results to the Julia runtime.

As a minimal demonstrator of this capability, this adds a Cassette-like `with_new_compiler` higher-order function, which will compile/execute its arguments with the currently loaded `Compiler` package. Unlike `@activate Compiler[:codegen]`, this change is not global and the cache is fully partitioned. This by itself is a very useful feature when developing Compiler code to be able to test the full end-to-end codegen behavior before the changes are capable of fully self-hosting.

A key enabler for this was the recent merging of #54899. This PR includes a hacky version of the second TODO left at the end of that PR, just to make everthing work end-to-end.

This PR is working end-to-end, but all three parts of it (the CodeInstance return from generated functions, the `with_new_compiler` feature, and the interpreter integration) need some additional cleanup. This PR is mostly intended as a discussion point for what that additional work needs to be.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56650/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56650/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2493605857","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56650#issuecomment-2493605857","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56650","id":2493605857,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6UoWfh","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-22T12:06:01Z","updated_at":"2024-11-22T12:06:01Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"> This by itself is a very useful feature when developing Compiler code to be able to test the full end-to-end codegen behavior before the changes are capable of fully self-hosting.

release"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-22T17:20:15Z","updated_at":"2024-11-22T18:53:29Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There appears to be an issue loading the LazyArraysBlockArraysExt package extension defined in [LazyArrays.jl](https://github.com/JuliaArrays/LazyArrays.jl) in Julia v1.11.1 and the [v1.11.2 pre-release](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56228), which doesn't appear in Julia v1.11.0 or Julia nightly.

Under certain conditions, when LazyArraysBlockArraysExt is being loaded in Julia v1.11.1 and the v1.11.2 pre-release, it fails with the error:
```julia
ERROR: LoadError: ArgumentError: Package LazyArraysBlockArraysExt does not have BlockArrays in its dependencies
```
as demonstrated in the CI logs of this PR: https://github.com/JuliaArrays/BlockArrays.jl/pull/433. 

I say "under certain conditions" because I'm only able to see the issue when it is run in Github Actions with the workflow defined in the BlockArrays repository, I haven't been able reproduce the issue locally (it must be related to the particular combination of packages that are loaded in the workflow). It might be related to #56426, #56204, #55939, but I'm raising a new issue since it isn't obvious to me if it is a new issue or a repeat of one of those. It looks most similar to #55939, however I removed BlockArrays from the dependency list of LazyArrays here: https://github.com/JuliaArrays/LazyArrays.jl/pull/347 and even after that the issue still shows up. We were hoping that #56228 would fix this issue but it doesn't seem like it has, since we tried testing against `1.11-nightly` in the workflow and see the same failure that we get when testing against `1.11.1` (but when we test against `1.11.0` or `nightly` the issue doesn't appear).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56656/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56656/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2494532505","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56656#issuecomment-2494532505","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56656","id":2494532505,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Ur4uZ","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-22T18:53:29Z","updated_at":"2024-11-22T18:53:29Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"I appears to be an issue loading the LazyArraysBlockArraysExt package extension defined in [LazyArrays.jl](https://github.com/JuliaArrays/LazyArrays.jl) in Julia v1.11.1 and the [v1.11.2 pre-release](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56228), which doesn't appear in Julia v1.11.0 or Julia nightly.

Under certain conditions, when LazyArraysBlockArraysExt is being loaded in Julia v1.11.1 and the v1.11.2 pre-release, it fails with the error:
```julia
ERROR: LoadError: ArgumentError: Package LazyArraysBlockArraysExt does not have BlockArrays in its dependencies
```
as demonstrated in the CI logs of this PR: https://github.com/JuliaArrays/BlockArrays.jl/pull/433. 

I say "under certain conditions" because I'm only able to see the issue when it is run in Github Actions with the workflow defined in the BlockArrays repository, I haven't been able reproduce the issue locally (it must be related to the particular combination of packages that are loaded in the workflow). It might be related to #56426, #56204, #55939, but I'm raising a new issue since it isn't obvious to me if it is a new issue or a repeat of one of those. It looks most similar to #55939, however I removed BlockArrays from the dependency list of LazyArrays here: https://github.com/JuliaArrays/LazyArrays.jl/pull/347 and even after that the issue still shows up. We were hoping that #56228 would fix this issue but it doesn't seem like it has, since we tried testing against `1.11-nightly` in the workflow and see the same failure that we get when testing against `1.11.1` (but when we test against `1.11.0` or `nightly` the issue doesn't +    function expand_indirect_dependencies(direct_deps)
+        function visit!(visited, node, all_deps)
+            if node in visited
+                return
+            end
+            push!(visited, node)
+            for dep in get(Set{Base.PkgId}, direct_deps, node)
+                if !(dep in all_deps)
+                    push!(all_deps, dep)
+                    visit!(visited, dep, all_deps)
+                end
+            end
+        end
+
+        indirect_deps = Dict{Base.PkgId, Set{Base.PkgId}}()
+        for package in keys(direct_deps)
+            # Initialize a set to keep track of all dependencies for 'package'
+            all_deps = Set{Base.PkgId}()
+            visited = Set{Base.PkgId}()
+            visit!(visited, package, all_deps)
+            # Update direct_deps with the complete set of dependencies for 'package'
+            indirect_deps[package] = all_deps
+        end
+        return indirect_deps
+    end
+
+    indirect_deps = expand_indirect_dependencies(depsmap)

```suggestion
            # In contrast to 1.11+, on 1.10 both "pkg → ext" and "ext → ext" dependency edges
            # are implied based on transitive dependencies.
            #
            # This condition is inconsistent for "ext → ext" edges, leading to dependency
            # cycles on 1.10, but this behavior is intentionally preserved for now to avoid
            # breaking packages that depend on this (bad) implicit behavior.
            #
            # See https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56204#issuecomment-2442652997
            # for the improved behavior this was replaced with in 1.11
            ext_loadable_in_pkg[pkg] = has_triggers && !is_trigger

There are some bugs now where extensions do not load when their package has been put into the sysimage. #52841 was made because it was common to get cycles otherwise but with https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55589 that should be much less of a problem.

+    function expand_indirect_dependencies(direct_deps)
+        function visit!(visited, node, all_deps)
+            if node in visited
+                return
+            end
+            push!(visited, node)
+            for dep in get(Set{Base.PkgId}, direct_deps, node)
+                if !(dep in all_deps)
+                    push!(all_deps, dep)
+                    visit!(visited, dep, all_deps)
+                end
+            end
+        end
+
+        indirect_deps = Dict{Base.PkgId, Set{Base.PkgId}}()
+        for package in keys(direct_deps)
+            # Initialize a set to keep track of all dependencies for 'package'
+            all_deps = Set{Base.PkgId}()
+            visited = Set{Base.PkgId}()
+            visit!(visited, package, all_deps)
+            # Update direct_deps with the complete set of dependencies for 'package'
+            indirect_deps[package] = all_deps
+        end
+        return indirect_deps
+    end
+

- [ ] #56306 <!-- InteractiveUtils.jl: fixes issue where subtypes resolves bindings and causes deprecation warnings  -->

- [ ] #55720 <!-- Fix `pkgdir` for extensions -->
- [ ] #55729 <!-- Avoid confounding compilation side effects of `@time_imports` -->
- [ ] #55589 <!-- prevent loading other extensions when precompiling an extension -->
- [ ] #55877 <!-- fix FileWatching designs and add workaround for a stat bug on Apple -->

release-1.11"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-24T12:28:26Z","updated_at":"2024-11-24T18:55:58Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56666","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56666","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56666.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56666.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"It is possible for an extension `ExtAB` to be loadable by one of its triggers, e.g. if `A` loads `B`. However, this loading is not supposed to happen during pre-compilation of `A`.

Getting this wrong means disagreeing with the scheduled pre-compile jobs (`A` is not scheduled to depend on or generate a cache file for `ExtAB` but accidentally attempts both) and leads to confusing errors about missing cache files.

We used to cover up this bad behavior w/ an erroneous cycle warning (fixed by #55910), but now we need to be sure this works.

is possible for an extension `ExtAB` to be loadable by one of its triggers, e.g. if `A` loads `B`. However, this loading is not supposed to happen during pre-compilation of `A`.

Getting this wrong means disagreeing with the scheduled pre-compile jobs (`A` is not scheduled to depend on or generate a cache file for `ExtAB` but accidentally attempts both) and leads to confusing errors about missing cache files.

We used to cover up this bad behavior w/ an erroneous cycle warning (fixed by #55910), but now we need to be sure this works.
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56666/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56666/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2496196926","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56666#issuecomment-2496196926","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56666","id":2496196926,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6UyPE-","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-24T19:37:22Z","updated_at":"2024-11-24T19:37:22Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"The 1.10 backport of this (e6c7c92e850d4bd1ed1a63493630477930d10797) went smoothly, but testing it exposed a new bug on 1.10:
```julia
$ ./julia --project=test/project/Extensions/Parent.jl -q
(Parent) pkg> precompile
Precompiling project...
  ✓ DepWithParentExt
  ✓ Parent
  2 dependencies successfully precompiled in 2 seconds

julia> using Parent
[ Info: Precompiling ParentExt [3290166e-fa45-5082-a21d-a36e4f051b06]
```

`] precompile` doesn't realize it needs to compile the extension because it doesn't have `Parent` in the old manifest format.

This is probably why @christiangnrd reported in https://github.com/JuliaConcurrent/Atomix.jl/issues/44#issuecomment-2495645840 that the bug fixed by this PR wasn't present on 1.10 - I only hit the loading bug here on 1.10 because I was using a new (1.11) Manifest which actually precompiles everything","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2496196926/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```

`] precompile` doesn't realize it needs to compile the extension because it doesn't have `Parent` in the old manifest format.

exposed a new bug on 1.10:
```julia
$ ./julia --project=test/project/Extensions/Parent.jl -q
(Parent) pkg> precompile
Precompiling project...
  ✓ DepWithParentExt
  ✓ Parent
  2 dependencies successfully precompiled in 2 seconds

julia> using Parent
[ Info: Precompiling ParentExt [3290166e-fa45-5082-a21d-a36e4f051b06]
```

`] precompile` doesn't realize it needs to compile the extension because it doesn't have `Parent` in the old manifest format.

 _Originally posted by @topolarity in [#56675](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56675#issuecomment-2496238280)_

Pkg is trying to look-up extension triggers in just `[weakdeps]` instead of both `[deps]` and `[weakdeps]`. This is not an issue on 1.11+, since the new `ExplicitEnv` code properly does the look-up properly

This is an important fix to get into 1.10, since our fix for https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56204 can sometimes force you to add triggers on `[deps]` instead of just `[weakdeps]`","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/4095/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/4095/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"repository_id":1644196,"push_id":21377594703,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/release-1.11","head":"4a7b1d1d3f5dba12485bf1ce96805b835c016910","before":"5ef18846715d33080ba40454fed141d30db91fdc","commits":[{"sha":"4a7b1d1d3f5dba12485bf1ce96805b835c016910","author":{"email":"a87f6485e8aa8163af7a61acec9c8b75e685fcec@users.noreply.github.com","name":"Cody Tapscott"},"message":"[release-1.11] Prevent pre-compilation package from triggering its own extensions (#56666) (#56676)

It is possible for an extension `ExtAB` to be loadable by one of its
triggers, e.g. if `A` loads `B`. However, this loading is not supposed
to happen during pre-compilation of `A`.

Getting this wrong means disagreeing with the scheduled pre-compile jobs
(`A` is not scheduled to depend on or generate a cache file for `ExtAB`
but accidentally attempts both) and leads to confusing errors about
missing cache files.

We used to cover up this bad behavior w/ an erroneous cycle warning
(fixed by #55910), but now we need to be sure this works.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/commits/4a7b1d1d3f5dba12485bf1ce96805b835c016910"}]}

```

master:

```julia-repl
~/JuliaPkgs/Pkg.jl
❯ julia +nightly --project -q

julia> VERSION
v"1.12.0-DEV.784"

julia> using Pkg
[ Info: Precompiling Pkg [44cfe95a-1eb2-52ea-b672-e2afdf69b78f] (cache misses: for different buildid (2), mismatched flags (2))

julia> pkg_ext = Base.PkgId(Base.uuid5(Base.UUID("44cfe95a-1eb2-52ea-b672-e2afdf69b78f"), "REPLExt"), "REPLExt")
REPLExt [e5eb5ef1-03cf-53a7-ae1d-5a66b08e832b]

julia> haskey(Base.loaded_modules, pkg_ext)
false
```

On 1.11.0-beta2:

```julia-repl
julia> using Pkg

julia> pkg_ext = Base.PkgId(Base.uuid5(Base.UUID("44cfe95a-1eb2-52ea-b672-e2afdf69b78f"), "REPLExt"), "REPLExt")
REPLExt [e5eb5ef1-03cf-53a7-ae1d-5a66b08e832b]

julia> haskey(Base.loaded_modules, pkg_ext)
true
```

running [this test from LinearAlgebra](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/9850a3881221a57a382e98c9b9ae2bf97ac3966d/stdlib/LinearAlgebra/test/diagonal.jl#L1445-L1453) in the REPL:
```julia
julia> using Test, LinearAlgebra

julia> @testset "zeros in kron with block matrices" begin
           D = Diagonal(1:4)
           B = reshape([ones(2,2), ones(3,2), ones(2,3), ones(3,3)], 2, 2)
           @test kron(D, B) == kron(Array(D), B)
           @test kron(B, D) == kron(B, Array(D))
           D2 = Diagonal([ones(2,2), ones(3,3)])
           @test kron(D, D2) == Diagonal([diag(D2); 2diag(D2)])
           @test kron(D2, D) == Diagonal([ones(2,2), fill(2.0,2,2), ones(3,3), fill(2.0,3,3)])
       end
```

fails with:
```julia
zeros in kron with block matrices: Test Failed at REPL[3]:7

```

revent extensions","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"It is possible for an extension `ExtAB` to be loadable by one of its triggers, e.g. if `A` loads `B`. However, this loading is not supposed to happen during pre-compilation of `A`.

Getting this wrong means disagreeing with the scheduled pre-compile jobs (`A` is not scheduled to depend on or generate a cache file for `ExtAB` but accidentally attempts both) and leads to confusing errors about missing cache files.

We used to cover up this bad behavior w/ an erroneous cycle warning (fixed by #55910), but now we need to be sure this works.

is possible for an extension `ExtAB` to be loadable by one of its triggers, e.g. if `A` loads `B`. However, this loading is not supposed to happen during pre-compilation of `A`.

Getting this wrong means disagreeing with the scheduled pre-compile jobs (`A` is not scheduled to depend on or generate a cache file for `ExtAB` but accidentally attempts both) and leads to confusing errors about missing cache files.

We used to cover up this bad behavior w/ an erroneous cycle warning (fixed by #55910), but now we need to be sure this works.

revent extensions","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"It is possible for an extension `ExtAB` to be loadable by one of its triggers, e.g. if `A` loads `B`. However, this loading is not supposed to happen during pre-compilation of `A`.

Getting this wrong means disagreeing with the scheduled pre-compile jobs (`A` is not scheduled to depend on or generate a cache file for `ExtAB` but accidentally attempts both) and leads to confusing errors about missing cache files.

We used to cover up this bad behavior w/ an erroneous cycle warning (fixed by #55910), but now we need to be sure this works.

PRs:
- [x] #55849 <!-- Mmap: fix grow! for non file IOs -->

- [x] #54433 <!-- Root globals in toplevel exprs -->
- [x] #55828 <!-- Fix some corner cases of `isapprox` with unsigned integers -->
- [x] #55890 <!-- Profile: fix order of fields in heapsnapshot & improve formatting -->
- [x] #55884 <!-- inference: add missing `TypeVar` handling for `instanceof_tfunc` -->
- [x] #55881 <!-- Install terminfo data under /usr/share/julia -->
- [x] #55909 <!-- do not intentionally suppress errors in precompile script from being reported or failing the result -->
- [x] #55355 <!-- relocation: account for trailing path separator in depot paths -->
- [x] #55917 <!-- fix rawbigints OOB issues -->
- [x] #55892 <!-- TOML: Avoid type-pirating `Base.TOML.Parser` -->
- [x] #55798 <!-- Broadcast binary ops involving strided triangular -->
- [x] #55919 <!-- Limit `@inbounds` to indexing in the dual-iterator branch in `copyto_unaliased!` -->

Contains multiple commits, manual intervention needed:
- [ ] #54009 <!-- allow extensions to trigger from packages in [deps] -->
- [ ] #55509 <!-- Fix cong implementation to be properly random and not just cycling. -->
- [ ] #55569 <!-- Add a docs section about loading/precomp/ttfx time tuning -->
- [ ] #55824 <!-- Replace regex package module checks with actual code checks -->

Non-merged PRs with backport label:
- [ ] #55932 <!-- REPL: make UndefVarError aware of imported modules -->
- [ ] #55910 <!-- Prevent extensions from blocking parallel pre-compilation -->
- [ ] #55908 <!-- add logic to prefer loading modules that are already loaded -->
- [ ] #55886 <!-- irrationals: restrict assume effects annotations to known types -->
- [ ] #55871 <!-- lowering: don't reverse handler order in `(pop-handler-list ...)` -->
- [ ] #55870 <!-- fix infinite recursion in `promote_type` for `Irrational` -->
- [ ] #55867 <!-- update `hash` doc string: `widen` not required any more -->
- [ ] #55851 <!-- [REPL] Fix #55850 by using `safe_realpath` instead of `abspath` in `projname` -->
- [ ] #55813 <!-- Check for conflicting `@ccallable` name before JIT registration -->
- [ ] #55743 <!-- doc: heap snapshot viewing -->
- [ ] #55741 <!-- Change annotations to use a NamedTuple -->
- [ ] #55534 <!-- Set stdlib sources as read-only during installation -->
- [ ] #55499 <!-- propagate the terminal's `displaysize` to the `IOContext` used by the REPL -->
- [ ] #55458 <!-- Allow for generically extracting unannotated string -->
- [ ] #55457 <!-- Make AnnotateChar equality consider annotations -->
- [ ] #55220 <!-- `isfile_casesensitive` fixes on Windows -->
- [ ] #55169 <!-- `propertynames` for SVD respects private argument -->
- [ ] #54457 <!-- Make `String(::Memory)` copy -->
- [ ] #53957 <!-- tweak how filtering is done for what packages should be precompiled -->
- [ ] #51479 <!-- prevent code loading from lookin in the versioned environment when building Julia -->
- [ ] #50813 <!-- More doctests for Sockets and capitalization fix -->
- [ ] #50157 <!-- improve docs for `@inbounds` and `Base.@propagate_inbounds` -->

`unwrap_macrocalls`","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"The implementation of `unwrap_macrocalls` has assumed that what `:macrocall` wraps is always an `Expr` object, but that is not necessarily correct:
```julia
julia> Base.@assume_effects :nothrow @show 42
ERROR: LoadError: TypeError: in typeassert, expected Expr, got a value of type Int64
Stacktrace:
 [1] unwrap_macrocalls(ex::Expr)
   @ Base ./expr.jl:906
 [2] var"@assume_effects"(__source__::LineNumberNode, __module__::Module, args::Vararg{Any})
   @ Base ./expr.jl:756
in expression starting at REPL[1]:1

seems to fix #55966 for me on v1.10:
```julia-repl
julia> run(`sudo mkdir -p $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> run(`sudo chmod go-x $(abspath(Sys.BUILD_STDLIB_PATH))`);

julia> using SuiteSparse
[ Info: Precompiling SuiteSparse [4607b0f0-06f3-5cda-b6b1-a6196a1729e9]

julia>
```

It's hard to make a proper test for this because it requires fiddling with the build directory, which for from-source builds is likely needed and you don't want to mess with it, and in general may require admin access.

I'll make a separate PR for `master`, as sketched in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55966#issuecomment-2389154120, I started from v1.10 because that's the only version where I can reliably reproduce the issue, and so test it locally easily.  Also, the code around these lines is changed in v1.11, so this would need a manual backport in any `require_stdlib`","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"As a recent offender, this tries to lay down some guidelines for the behavior that stdlibs and the callers of `require_stdlib` must adhere too to avoid \"duplicate stdlib\" bugs.\r\n\r\nThese bugs are The second copy of StyledStrings contains a default "FACES" dictionary (w/o the Markdown faces) and then overrides Base.write(::IO, ::Base.AnnotatedString) so that any old AnnotatedStrings floating about start seeing the "FACES" only from the second loaded copy of StyledStrings. In particular, the AnnotatedString's created by Markdown are suddenly missing their Face definitions.

Mis-behavior like this is really easy to get with `require_stdlib` + type-piracy, so I've started to document the rules of the game in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56005

The part that matters is:
>  A stdlib must not access any global state that may differ between stdlib copies in type-pirated methods

Given that guideline, I think we only have a few options here:
 - Eliminate the type piracy: Make `AnnotatedString` a different type for each of the StyledStrings copies (move it out of Base)
 - Avoid `FACES` duplication: StyledStrings needs to move into the sysimage, or `require_stdlib` needs to be banned
 - Include a unique, type-based identifier for Annotations so that StyledStrings can resolve `FACES` in a non-type-pirated method
 - Maintain a reference to the relevant `FACES` dict in the AnnotatedString

The basic problem is that a type-pirated method must be simultaneously correct for all copies of a stdlib.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/StyledStrings.jl/issues/comments/2395073542/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

the relevant definition of "type piracy" here is "You can dispatch to this Method even without using any types defined in the package/stdlib"

This unfortunately means that:
```julia
function Base.foo(x::Ref{T}) where T <: MyAbstractType
     # ...
end
```

is committing a (tiny slice of) type piracy even if the stdlib owns `MyAbstractType`, because `foo(::Ref{Union{}})` can dispatch to this method.

That kind of dispatch is a real (albeit rare) problem for stdlib correct-ness though, and the current fix would be to define `foo(x::Ref{Union{}})` upstream, so that it blocks dispatch to this method by specificity.

Just one example of the subtleties here...","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2395084328/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":56034,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56034","id":2111791690,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5932JK","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56034","number":56034,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"array: inline `convert` where possible","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
foo(v::Vector{Int}, @nospecialize(x::Integer)) = push!(v, x)
```

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{Int}, Integer))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ %1 = Main.push!(v, x)::Vector{Int64}
└──      return %1
) => Vector{Int64}
```

improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
foo(v::Vector{Int}, @nospecialize(x::Integer)) = push!(v, x)
```

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{Int}, Integer))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ %1 = Main.push!(v, x)::Vector{Int64}
└──      return %1
) => Vector{Int64}
```

With this PR, the above dynamic call is fully statically resolved and inlined (and therefore `--trim` compatible)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56034/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56034/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2398001817","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034#issuecomment-2398001817","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56034","id":2398001817,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6O7pqZ","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-07T22:04:53Z","updated_at":"2024-10-07T22:04:53Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"BTW @aviatesk it's unrelated to this PR really, I'm a bit surprised that we have so many `@assume_effects :noub` here around convert(...) calls that we don't control. This requires the expected code churn to `strings/annotated.jl`, and some changes to the StyledStrings and JuliaSyntaxHighlighting libraries.

Closes #55249 and closes ```julia
3 ── %9  = (isa)(%4, @NamedTuple{x::Int64, y})::Bool
└───       goto #5 if not %9
4 ── %11 = π (%4, @NamedTuple{x::Int64, y})
└───       goto #6
5 ── %13 = (Tuple{Int64, Any})(%4)::Tuple{Int64, Any}
│    %14 = (getfield)(%13, 1)::Int64
│    %15 = (getfield)(%13, 2)::Any
│    %16 = %new(@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}, %14, %15)::@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}
```

is now:
```julia
3 ── %9  = builtin (isa)(%4, @NamedTuple{x::Int64, y})::Bool
└───       goto #5 if not %9
4 ── %11 = π (%4, @NamedTuple{x::Int64, y})
└───       goto #6
5 ── %13 = dynamic (Tuple{Int64, Any})(%4)::Tuple{Int64, Any}
│    %14 = builtin (getfield)(%13, 1)::Int64
│    %15 = builtin (getfield)(%13, 2)::Any
│    %16 = %new(@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}, %14, %15)::@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}

[This issue](https://github.com/JuliaGeo/Proj.jl/issues/104) contains an MWE reporting a possible dynamic dispatch occuring in a @ccall function in the package Proj.jl. However, upon investigation using other methods, it was determined that there was not an actual dynamic dispatch that occurred, even though AllocCheck.jl says that there is.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81","id":2451734249,"node_id":"I_kwDOKTlkfc6SIn7p","number":81,"title":"`multiple call sites` obscures allocation origin","user":{"login":"danielwe","id":5809017,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU4MDkwMTc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5809017?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe","html_url":"https://github.com/danielwe","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/danielwe/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-06T21:38:25Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T05:13:25Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Allocations reported by AllocCheck often have stack traces that only contain stack frames from Julia core/Base (array/dict/set constructors, locks, gc code, etc.) before terminating with `multiple call sites`. This is unhelpful for debugging and makes it hard to filter out allocations that a developer might consider benign.

Could these cases instead be split into multiple allocations, one for each origin, each with a stack trace going back to the top-level call?

As an example, see the code below, where a simple log statement results in 95 reported allocations, and an attempt to filter out the logging-related ones only removes 30 of them.

```julia-repl
julia> using AllocCheck

julia> f() = @warn "Watch out!"
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> allocs = check_allocs(f, ());

julia> length(allocs)
95

julia> allocs_not_from_loggingJL = filter(allocs) do alloc
           for frame in alloc.backtrace
               (frame.file == Symbol("./logging.jl")) && return false
           end
           return true
       end;

julia> length(allocs_not_from_loggingJL)
65

julia> allocs_not_from_loggingJL[1]  # example of stack trace terminating with "multiple call sites"
Allocation of String in ./strings/string.jl:90
  | @eval _string_n(n::Integer) = $(Expr(:foreigncall, QuoteNode(:jl_alloc_string), Ref{String}, Expr(:call, Expr(:core, :svec), :Csize_t), 1, QuoteNode((:ccall,0xe)), :(convert(Csize_t, n))))

Stacktrace:
 [1] _string_n
   @ ./strings/string.jl:90 [inlined]
 [2] StringVector
   @ ./iobuffer.jl:32 [inlined]
 [3] _similar_data
   @ ./iobuffer.jl:142 [inlined]
 [4] ensureroom
   @ ./iobuffer.jl:337 [inlined]
 [5] unsafe_write(to::IOBuffer, p::Ptr{UInt8}, nb::UInt64)
   @ Base ./iobuffer.jl:458
 [6] multiple call sites
   @ unknown:0
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/comments/2398855059","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81#issuecomment-2398855059","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/81","id":2398855059,"node_id":"IC_kwDOKTlkfc6O-5-T","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-08T05:13:23Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T05:13:23Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"It may not be necessary to show all of the paths at once - We should probably try following at least one of the paths back to the entry-point
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/comments/2398855059/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"repository_id":691627133,"push_id":20602024025,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/ct/unresolved","head":"f7ab19c652d5c6456b0fe70a471db272a161f0d0","before":"1786643e23caf81ad9ace26ec76efb7d180f734a","commits":[{"sha":"f7ab19c652d5c6456b0fe70a471db272a161f0d0","author":{"email":"308377d01bcb9d4e526ee30856be1f850fe7e000@tapscott.me","name":"Cody Tapscott"},"message":"Add `UnresolvedRuntimeCall` for unresolved function calls

These can occasionally happen due to known incomplete spots in our call
resolution (https://github.com/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/issues/69) or due
to changes in the optimizer, etc.

Rather than conservatively calling these "dynamic dispatches", this adds
a new error type that encourages the user to file a bug upstream, since a
feature-complete AllocCheck should never encounter them.","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/AllocCheck.jl/commits/f7ab19c652d5c6456b0fe70a471db272a161f0d0"}]}

possible","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
const NT = @NamedTuple{x::Int,y::Any}
foo(v::Vector{NT}, x::Int, @nospecialize(y)) = push!(v, (; x, y))
```

The `(; x, y)` is slightly poorly-typed here. It could have any type for its `.y` field before it is converted inside the `push!` to a NamedTuple with `y::Any`

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{NT}, Int, Any))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ ...
│   %4 = %new(%3, x, y)::NamedTuple{(:x, :y), <:Tuple{Int64, Any}}
│   %5 = Main.push!(v, %4)::Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
└──      return %5
) => Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
```

With this PR, the above dynamic call is fully statically resolved and inlined (and therefore `--trim` compatible)","created_at":"2024-10-07T22:02:59Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T13:31:26Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"c9c23defa61afedcc1e196adf9ca1212b4d86e7b","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[{"login":"oscardssmith","id":11729272,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzI5Mjcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11729272?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith","html_url":"https://github.com/oscardssmith","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":62693664,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MjY5MzY2NA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/arrays","name":"arrays","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":"[a, `--trim`)","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-08T14:09:29Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T14:19:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56045","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56045","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56045.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56045.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"The intention here is to clearly signal when a feature is "not yet fully implemented" vs. "feature-complete and in pre-release" vs. "fully released and ready for production use"

The only feature gated behind this right now is `--trim`. Trim has its core functionality implemented (and folks seem to be enjoying it!) but the deployment / linking story in particular is still in its very early stages, esp. because our existing techniques for, e.g., pre-loading `libunwind`, `libstdc++`, etc. no longer work in a shared library context.

r, r, a, y, s]"},{"id":171885699,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNzE4ODU2OTk=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/maths","name":"maths","color":"93755D","default":false,"description":"Mathematical functions"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-10-06T15:33:40Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T14:58:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"```julia
a = rand(100, 100)
b = a
isapprox(a, b)
```
does the same long computation that `isapprox` does if `b = rand(100, 100)` instead.

This may be intentional, but I can't think of an explanation. I'm suspicious because it's also the case that 
 ```julia
a == b
```` 
does not take a fast exit if `a === b`.

EDIT: I'm trying to find an example that shows that `isapprox` should check `a === b`.  But the first things I try do not support this.  Identity is not checked by the method for `==` for `a::BigInt`, `b::BigInt`. Neither is it checked for `AbstractArray`, nor for the special case `BitIntegerArray{1}`.  I can't believe all of these are oversights. There must be a reason.

Maybe the caller is expected to make this check. Say I'm sampling with replacement from a multiset and comparing pairs of samples. Then I have to remember to check for identity if I want this performance gain. Here is an example that's contrived to make the cost of comparing samples large

```julia
using BenchmarkTools

const a = ones(100);
const b = a;
const c = ones(100);

const v = [a, b, c]

cmp_samp() = cmp(v[rand(1:3)], v[rand(1:3)])
mycmp_samp() = mycmp(v[rand(1:3)], v[rand(1:3)])

mycmp(x, y) = x === y ? 0 : cmp(x, y)

@btime sum(mycmp_samp() for _ in 1:100);
@btime sum(cmp_samp() for _ in 1:100);
```

```shell
  3.484 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  8.659 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```

s]"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2024-10-07T22:02:59Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T15:08:43Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56034","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
const NT = @NamedTuple{x::Int,y::Any}
foo(v::Vector{NT}, x::Int, @nospecialize(y)) = push!(v, (; x, y))
```

The `(; x, y)` is slightly poorly-typed here. It could have any type for its `.y` field before it is converted inside the `push!` to a NamedTuple with `y::Any`

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{NT}, Int, Any))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ ...
│   %4 = %new(%3, x, y)::NamedTuple{(:x, :y), <:Tuple{Int64, Any}}
│   %5 = Main.push!(v, %4)::Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
└──      return %5
) => Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
```

With this PR, the above dynamic call is fully statically resolved and inlined (and therefore `--trim` compatible)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56034/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":2,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56034/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2400119142","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56034#issuecomment-2400119142","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56034","id":2400119142,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6PDulm","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-08T15:08:42Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T15:08:42Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"That doesn't sound the same to me. Somehow `foo` ended up with better effects than its callee `bar` but its effects are also strictly better than any assumptions we provided via `@_noub_if_noinbounds_meta`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56058/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56058/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2401118415","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56058#issuecomment-2401118415","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56058","id":2401118415,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6PHijP","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-09T01:57:03Z","updated_at":"2024-10-09T01:57:03Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"OK I think this is fine - I was mis-understanding how `:noub_if_noinbounds` works

It's a bit unique, because the condition isn't inductive by default: Inlining / invoking a function hard-codes a decision with respect to the elision of its `@boundscheck` code.

If it's inlined into an `@inbounds` code region, then it does not have its `@boundscheck` code and it is `!:noub`. If it's called or inlined outside of an `@inbounds` code region, then the `@boundscheck` code is not elided and it's `:noub` if it was `:noub_if_noinbounds`. The exception to this is `@propagate_inbounds`, which means that the `@boundscheck` code in any inlined callees becomes `@boundscheck` code for the caller. That _does_ make the definition inductive, which is how I originally thought it worked.

possible","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
const NT = @NamedTuple{x::Int,y::Any}
foo(v::Vector{NT}, x::Int, @nospecialize(y)) = push!(v, (; x, y))
```

The `(; x, y)` is slightly poorly-typed here. It could have any type for its `.y` field before it is converted inside the `push!` to a NamedTuple with `y::Any`

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{NT}, Int, Any))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ ...
│   %4 = %new(%3, x, y)::NamedTuple{(:x, :y), <:Tuple{Int64, Any}}
│   %5 = Main.push!(v, %4)::Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
└──      return %5
) => Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
```

With this PR, the above dynamic call is fully statically resolved and inlined (and therefore `--trim` compatible)","created_at":"2024-10-07T22:02:59Z","updated_at":"2024-10-09T02:17:59Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"aeda5bd4f408e7cb81bfe7351ff1969d42613531","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[{"login":"oscardssmith","id":11729272,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzI5Mjcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11729272?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith","html_url":"https://github.com/oscardssmith","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":62693664,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MjY5MzY2NA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/arrays","name":"arrays","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":"[a, possible","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
const NT = @NamedTuple{x::Int,y::Any}
foo(v::Vector{NT}, x::Int, @nospecialize(y)) = push!(v, (; x, y))
```

The `(; x, y)` is slightly poorly-typed here. It could have any type for its `.y` field before it is converted inside the `push!` to a NamedTuple with `y::Any`

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{NT}, Int, Any))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ ...
│   %4 = %new(%3, x, y)::NamedTuple{(:x, :y), <:Tuple{Int64, Any}}
│   %5 = Main.push!(v, %4)::Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
└──      return %5
) => Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
```

With this PR, the above dynamic call is fully statically resolved and inlined (and therefore `--trim` compatible)","created_at":"2024-10-07T22:02:59Z","updated_at":"2024-10-09T12:25:18Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"09861c6a491adf00f3a6b752cfe05737677e7377","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[{"login":"oscardssmith","id":11729272,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNzI5Mjcy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11729272?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith","html_url":"https://github.com/oscardssmith","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oscardssmith/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":62693664,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MjY5MzY2NA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/arrays","name":"arrays","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":"[a, This still feels like a mis-design to me - `@propagate_inbounds` is used to propagate an assumption on the inbounded-ness of your arguments to the inbounded-ness of the arguments on a callee.

But a `set/getindex` implementation might do `x[i]` in multiple places and they may not all be an x/i that can legally assume inbounded-ness. Is this expected, or a measurement error in `@allocations` maybe?

### MWE
```Julia
function push_allocs(n)
    x = Int[]
    as = Pair{Int, Int}[]
    for i in 1:n
        a = @allocations push!(x, i)
        if a > 0
            push!(as, i => a)
        end
    end
    as
end

push_allocs(10_000)
```

### Julia 1.10 result
```Julia
8-element Vector{Pair{Int64, Int64}}:
    1 => 1
    9 => 1
   42 => 1
  175 => 1
  708 => 1
 1820 => 1
 4095 => 1
 8702 => 1
```

### Julia 1.11 result
```Julia
8-element Vector{Pair{Int64, Int64}}:
    1 => 1
    9 => 1
   42 => 1
  175 => 2
  708 => 2
 1820 => 2
 4095 => 2
 8702 => 2
```

possible","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
const NT = @NamedTuple{x::Int,y::Any}
foo(v::Vector{NT}, x::Int, @nospecialize(y)) = push!(v, (; x, y))
```

The `(; x, y)` is slightly poorly-typed here. It could have any type for its `.y` field before it is converted inside the `push!` to a NamedTuple with `y::Any`

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{NT}, Int, Any))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ ...
│   %4 = %new(%3, x, y)::NamedTuple{(:x, :y), <:Tuple{Int64, Any}}
│   %5 = Main.push!(v, %4)::Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
└──      return %5
) => Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
```

With this PR, the above dynamic call is fully statically resolved and inlined (and therefore `--trim` Is this expected, or a measurement error in `@allocations` maybe?

### MWE
```Julia
function push_allocs(n)
    x = Int[]
    as = Pair{Int, Int}[]
    for i in 1:n
        a = @allocations push!(x, i)
        if a > 0
            push!(as, i => a)
        end
    end
    as
end

push_allocs(10_000)
```

### Julia 1.10 result
```Julia
8-element Vector{Pair{Int64, Int64}}:
    1 => 1
    9 => 1
   42 => 1
  175 => 1
  708 => 1
 1820 => 1
 4095 => 1
 8702 => 1
```

### Julia 1.11 result
```Julia
8-element Vector{Pair{Int64, Int64}}:
    1 => 1
    9 => 1
   42 => 1
  175 => 2
  708 => 2
 1820 => 2
 4095 => 2
 8702 => 2
```

possible","user":{"login":"topolarity","id":84105208,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg0MTA1MjA4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/84105208?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity","html_url":"https://github.com/topolarity","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/topolarity/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This improves a common scenario, where someone wants to `push!` a poorly-typed object onto a well-typed Vector.

For example:
```julia
const NT = @NamedTuple{x::Int,y::Any}
foo(v::Vector{NT}, x::Int, @nospecialize(y)) = push!(v, (; x, y))
```

The `(; x, y)` is slightly poorly-typed here. It could have any type for its `.y` field before it is converted inside the `push!` to a NamedTuple with `y::Any`

Without this PR, the dispatch for this `push!` cannot be inferred:
```julia
julia> code_typed(foo, (Vector{NT}, Int, Any))[1]
 CodeInfo(
1 ─ ...
│   %4 = %new(%3, x, y)::NamedTuple{(:x, :y), <:Tuple{Int64, Any}}
│   %5 = Main.push!(v, %4)::Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
└──      return %5
) => Vector{@NamedTuple{x::Int64, y}}
```

With this PR, the above dynamic call is fully statically resolved and inlined (and therefore `--trim` compatible)","created_at":"2024-10-07T22:02:59Z","updated_at":"2024-10-10T14:02:10Z","closed_at":null,"merged_at":null,"merge_commit_sha":"79393860ef30e3ddaf7a7ee63598e7dfd4f52852","assignee":null,"assignees":[],"requested_reviewers":[{"login":"vtjnash","id":330950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzMDk1MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/330950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash","html_url":"https://github.com/vtjnash","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"requested_teams":[],"labels":[{"id":62693664,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MjY5MzY2NA==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/arrays","name":"arrays","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":"[a,
